title: Built-in SDK and Roslyn analyzer activation
description: Lead with the analyzer stack that already ships in the .NET SDK, compiler, and Roslyn editor tooling so teams get actionable feedback in the editor, build output, and CI before adding anything custom.
enableHow: Use EnableNETAnalyzers, AnalysisLevel, AnalysisMode, CodeAnalysisTreatWarningsAsErrors, and EnforceCodeStyleInBuild in the project file, then set per-rule severities with dotnet_diagnostic.<ID>.severity in .editorconfig.
encouragesPattern: turn built-in analyzers on before adding custom ones
roll out severities deliberately across editor and CI

title: Modern C# pattern suggestions
description: Official IDE analyzers suggest newer language patterns a little at a time, including collection expressions, pattern matching, using declarations, and primary constructors.
enableHow: Configure style options in .editorconfig, turn on EnforceCodeStyleInBuild when you want build-time enforcement, and promote individual IDE rules with dotnet_diagnostic.<ID>.severity.

title: Style, cleanup, and consistency guidance
description: Official style rules keep null checks, namespace shape, and other everyday cleanup choices consistent so broader modernization stays readable.
enableHow: Keep the preferred style options in .editorconfig, use EnforceCodeStyleInBuild for build enforcement, and raise or lower individual IDE severities as the repo settles on conventions.

title: API usage and code-quality guidance
description: The SDK's CA analyzers bring performance, reliability, security, and API-usage guidance into normal compilation so issues surface before review.
enableHow: Keep EnableNETAnalyzers on, choose AnalysisLevel and AnalysisMode for the rollout you want, and use CodeAnalysisTreatWarningsAsErrors plus per-rule severities to decide what CI blocks.

title: Platform compatibility guidance
description: CA1416 and related platform analysis help teams adopt platform-specific APIs safely by requiring target-framework context, attributes, or runtime guards.
enableHow: The platform analyzer rides with the official analyzer stack; for older targets you can set AnalysisLevel and dotnet_code_quality.enable_platform_analyzer_on_pre_net5_target, then tune dotnet_diagnostic.CA1416.severity.

title: Nullable and flow-analysis guidance
description: Compiler nullability analysis keeps API contracts honest by warning on maybe-null dereferences and missing initialization before objects escape construction.
enableHow: Enable nullable analysis with Nullable in the project file, then use nullable annotations, attributes, and per-warning severity choices to tighten the rollout over time.

title: CSE001 — Prefer string interpolation
description: Planned follow-on analyzer that spots string.Format-style code and points developers to the site's string interpolation guidance.
enableHow: Coming soon as a local-only package preview after a repo build; not published to NuGet.

title: CSE002 — Prefer using declarations
description: Planned follow-on analyzer that nudges disposable-scope boilerplate toward using declarations and then links back to the site's feature guide.
enableHow: Coming soon as a local-only package preview after a repo build; not published to NuGet.

title: CSE003 — Prefer collection expressions
description: Planned follow-on analyzer that highlights older collection initializer shapes and points to collection expressions when the target code is ready.
enableHow: Coming soon as a local-only package preview after a repo build; not published to NuGet.
